caption.full:
William Wisher, co-writer of Terminator 2, has died at 71.

Wisher, a boyhood friend of James Cameron, also helped develop The Terminator. He recalled the daunting decision to make Arnold Schwarzenegger's T-800 a hero in the sequel. Wisher also served as an executive producer on Live Free or Die Hard.

What moment of Terminator 2 stays closest to you now that William Wisher isn’t here to see it again?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#MovieNews #Terminator #WilliamWisher #JamesCameron
caption.firstComment: Thinking of William Wisher today — the way he trusted Arnold’s quiet humanity in T2 still reshaped sci-fi. Which line from the film feels like it’s carrying his voice every time you watch?

facebook.text: William Wisher, co-writer of Terminator 2: Judgment Day and boyhood friend of James Cameron, has died at 71. He helped develop The Terminator and recalled the daunting decision to make Arnold Schwarzenegger's T-800 a hero. Share your memories of his work.

youtube.title: William Wisher Dies at 71; Co-wrote Terminator 2
youtube.description:
William Wisher, co-writer of The Terminator and Terminator 2: Judgment Day, died at age 71. He was a boyhood friend of James Cameron and helped develop the original film. Wisher also served as an executive producer on Live Free or Die Hard.

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

Watch more: https://thescreenreport.com/celebrity/william-wisher-co-writer-of-terminator-2-judgment-day-dies-at-71/
